title: Melissa Virus
description: Classified as a worm, it was created by a hacker who named the malware for a Florida dancer., Perhaps the most famous example of malware. It masqueraded as a love letter in email format.

title: Conficker Virus
description: One of the most destructive worms in history is still active and is responsible for 1 percent of all phishing emails sent.

title: Klez Virus
description: One of the most destructive worms in history is still active and is responsible for 1 percent of all phishing emails sent.

title: Melissa Virus
description: Classified as a worm, it was created by a hacker who named the malware for a Florida dancer., Perhaps the most famous example of malware. It masqueraded as a love letter in email format.

title: Klez Virus
description: It threatened devices with its advanced design and was considered one of the most destructive (and long-lasting) worms to infiltrate Windows devices. The virus created what was known as a botnet, or a network of connected computers controlled by one person.

title: Mydoom Virus
description: Yet another worm that forwarded messages to users’ address books. It first appeared in 2001 and came with multiple tricks to fool unsuspecting users.
